options: There is someone who likes everyone
Everyone likes at least one person
hint: Read it outside-in: ∃x first (one fixed person), then ∀y (that one person likes all y).
explanation: ∃x∀y(Likes(x, y)): there is ONE fixed person x who likes every y. Contrast ∀y∃x, where the liker can differ per person. And note 'someone is liked by everyone' would be ∃y∀x(Likes(x, y)) — the arguments are swapped. Quantifier ORDER changes the meaning.
